Logic Analysis: config.py
Contains configuration class with database URI, secret key, threshold for stock alerts (DEFAULT 5), expiration check intervals. Shared by all modules.
app/models.py
Contains SQLAlchemy ORM models: BloodUnit, TransfusionRequest, Reservation, StockAlert. These models are imported by routes, matching_service, scheduler, and forms.
app/forms.py
Contains Flask-WTF forms: AddBloodUnitForm and TransfusionRequestForm. Uses models for choices (ABO types, Rh). Imported by routes.
app/matching_service.py

Contains MatchingService class with methods find_compatible_units, reserve_units, auto_match_and_reserve. Depends on models (BloodUnit, TransfusionRequest, Reservation) and uses database session. Imported by routes and scheduler.
app/scheduler.py
Contains Scheduler class with jobs: expire_units_job, release_unconfirmed_reservations, check_and_raise_alerts. Uses models and matching_service (for alert creation). Started from main.py.
app/routes.py

Contains Flask blueprints for inventory, requests, dashboard, alerts. Uses models, forms, matching_service, and scheduler (for manual trigger?). Imports from app/models, app/forms, app/matching_service.
templates/base.html
Base Jinja2 template with Bootstrap, blocks for content, scripts, styles. Shared by all page templates.
templates/dashboard.html
Dashboard page template extending base.html. Displays stock summary, expiring soon, open requests, active alerts. Uses dashboard data passed from routes.
templates/inventory.html
Inventory management page: list of blood units with filters, add form, expire button.
templates/requests.html
Transfusion request page: list of requests with status, new request form.
templates/alerts.html
Alert history page showing active and resolved alerts.
static/css/style.css
Custom CSS styles for the dashboard.
static/js/main.js
Optional JavaScript for interactive elements (e.g., confirmation dialogs, auto-refresh).
main.py
Application entry point. Creates Flask app, registers blueprints, configures APScheduler, initializes database. Imports from config, app.routes, app.scheduler.
Full API spec:
openapi: 3.0.0
info:
  title: Blood Bank Management API
  description: RESTful endpoints for inventory and transfusion request management.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /inventory/add:
    post:
      summary: Add a new blood unit
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/x-www-form-urlencoded:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                abo_type:
                  type: string
                  enum: [A, B, AB, O]
                rh_factor:
                  type: string
                  enum: [positive, negative]
                collection_date:
                  type: string
                  format: date
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to dashboard with success message
        '400':
          description: Validation error
  /requests/new:
    post:
      summary: Create a new transfusion request (with auto-matching)
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/x-www-form-urlencoded:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                abo_type:
                  type: string
                  enum: [A, B, AB, O]
                rh_factor:
                  type: string
                  enum: [positive, negative]
                quantity:
                  type: integer
                  minimum: 1
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect to dashboard with result message
        '400':
          description: Validation error
  /dashboard:
    get:
      summary: Retrieve dashboard data
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page with stock summary, expiring units, open requests, active alerts
  /inventory:
    get:
      summary: List all blood units with filters
      parameters:
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [available, reserved, issued, expired]
        - name: abo_type
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [A, B, AB, O]
        - name: rh_factor
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [positive, negative]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML inventory page
  /requests:
    get:
      summary: List all transfusion requests
      parameters:
        - name: status
          in: query
          schema:
            type: string
            enum: [pending, fulfilled, cancelled]
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML requests page
  /alerts:
    get:
      summary: List all stock alerts (active and resolved)
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML alerts page
  /inventory/{id}/issue:
    post:
      summary: Issue a reserved blood unit
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect
  /requests/{id}/cancel:
    post:
      summary: Cancel a pending transfusion request and release reserved units
      parameters:
        - name: id
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: integer
      responses:
        '302':
          description: Redirect
Shared Knowledge: Database tables (models.py) define the core data structures. All modules share the same SQLAlchemy instance (db) imported from app.models. Matching rules: ABO compatibility follows standard transfusion guidelines (O is universal donor, AB universal recipient); Rh-negative can only receive Rh-negative, Rh-positive can receive both. Stock alert threshold defined in config.py (default 5). APScheduler runs three background jobs: expire units (hourly), release unconfirmed reservations (every 10 min), check stock alerts (every 5 min). Templates use Jinja2 with Bootstrap 5. Static files are served from /static/.
Anything UNCLEAR: Role-based access and authentication are not implemented in this version. For production, Flask-Login and role decorators can be added without changing architecture. The alert mechanism currently only shows in dashboard; future integrations (email/SMS) would be additional services. Rh compatibility clarification: implemented as per standard transfusion rules as described.
